name: Caitanya Mahaprabhu
dates_notes: Multiple near-contemporary biographies fix the dates with unusual precision. The *Caitanya-Bhāgavata* of Vṛndāvana-Dāsa (composed c. 1542–1555, within a generation of Caitanya's *prayāṇa*) and the *Caitanya-Caritāmṛta* of Kṛṣṇadāsa Kavirāja (c. 1612–1615) are the principal sources, with the *Caitanya-Maṅgala* of Locana-Dāsa and the *Murāri-Gupta-Kaḍacā* (an eyewitness diary attributed to a member of the Navadvīpa circle) as collateral attestation. Birth (Phālguna-pūrṇimā 1407 Śaka = 18 February 1486 CE, Navadvīpa) and *prayāṇa* (1455 Śaka = 1534 CE, Purī) are recorded across all three *carita*-texts. Modern reconstruction: Sushil Kumar De, *Early History of the Vaiṣṇava Faith and Movement in Bengal* (Firma KLM, 2nd ed. 1961), chs. I–II.

dates_evidence.description: Mughal-period manuscript colophons attest his biographical span.

lineage_polemical.note: Reported debate-tradition with the Nimbārka-line scholar Keśava-Kāśmīrī.

core_thesis:
Caitanya wrote no bhāṣya. His position is preserved in the *Śikṣāṣṭakam*, in the biographical literature (principally Kṛṣṇadāsa Kavirāja's *Caitanya-Caritāmṛta*), and in the systematic theology of the Six Gosvāmīs of Vṛndāvana, who codify the doctrine as *acintya-bhedābheda*. The interpretive label is post-Caitanyan but doctrinally faithful, and the Gosvāmīs treat themselves as drawing out what was already settled in his teaching.

The load-bearing concept is *acintya-śakti*. Brahman, identified with Kṛṣṇa as *svayaṃ-bhagavān*, stands to the *jīva* and the world in a relation of simultaneous *bheda* and *abheda*. The qualifier *acintya* — inconceivable, not derivable by *tarka* alone — does specific work here, and it is critical to read it correctly. It is not the claim that the school has given up on logic, nor a retreat from argument when objection threatens. It is a positive metaphysical commitment about the limits of dialectic relative to a particular ontological structure: the relation between Bhagavān and his eternal *śaktis*.

Jīva Gosvāmī states the point in *Tattva-Sandarbha* §51 ff. Dialectical reasoning operates by *bheda*–*abheda* exclusion: of two terms, either they are non-identical (and the relation is *bheda*) or they are identical (and the relation is *abheda*); dialectic adjudicates which. *Acintya-śakti* is the school's name for the structural feature by which, in the relation of *śakti* to *śaktimat*, both *bheda* and *abheda* hold non-trivially in the same respect. Dialectic is not abolished. It is acknowledged as the wrong instrument for adjudicating one specific case, and the warrant for that acknowledgment is *śabda* and the experiential testimony of *bhakti*, not the suspension of argument.

The ontology that follows: Bhagavān alone is *svayaṃ-bhagavān* (*Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* 1.3.28, *kṛṣṇas tu bhagavān svayam*), and his three *śaktis* — *svarūpa* / *cit*, *taṭasthā* / *jīva*, *bahiraṅgā* / *māyā* — are simultaneously identical with him in substance and irreducibly distinct as *śaktis* (*Bhagavat-Sandarbha* §16, on *Viṣṇu-Purāṇa* 6.7.61's *parā* / *kṣetrajña* / *avidyā* triad). Soteriologically, *bhakti-rasa* with *nāma-saṅkīrtana* as its principal mode is the operative path ([*Śikṣāṣṭakam* 1](cite://caitanya/shikshashtakam/1), *ceto-darpaṇa-mārjanam*). Liberation is eternal devotional participation in Kṛṣṇa-līlā, structured by *rasa* — *śānta*, *dāsya*, *sakhya*, *vātsalya*, *mādhurya* — and is never the dissolution of devotee into deity (the typology fixed at [*Bhakti-Rasāmṛta-Sindhu* 2.5](cite://rupa-gosvami/bhakti-rasamrta-sindhu/2.5), *uttara-vibhāga*; *jīva* as eternal servant at [*Śikṣāṣṭakam* 5](cite://caitanya/shikshashtakam/5), *ayi nanda-tanuja kiṅkaram*).

engaged_works.title: Shikshashtakam
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored eight-verse hymn — the only text indisputably authored by Caitanya himself.
engaged_works.summary: The *Śikṣāṣṭakam* is the only text indisputably composed by Caitanya. Eight Sanskrit verses, preserved within Kṛṣṇadāsa Kavirāja's *Caitanya-Caritāmṛta*, do disproportionate doctrinal work. The first verse establishes the supremacy of *nāma-saṅkīrtana* — chanting the divine name — as the means of cleansing consciousness in the present *yuga*. The third lays out the devotional virtues by which alone the chanting bears fruit: humility lower than the blade of grass (*tṛṇād api sunīcena*), forbearance like the tree (*taror iva sahiṣṇunā*), absence of demand for self-honor, honor given to others. The fifth states the *jīva*'s status as eternal servant of Kṛṣṇa (*ayi nanda-tanuja kiṅkaraṃ*). The sixth and seventh treat the experiential intensity of *viraha* — separation from Kṛṣṇa — as itself a high mode of *bhakti*. The fourth refuses all selfish ends, including wealth, followers, and even *mokṣa* construed as personal release, in favor of *ahaitukī bhakti*: causeless, motiveless devotion. The Gosvāmīs read these verses as the doctrinal seed of the systematic Gauḍīya theology of *rasa* and *prema* they go on to construct, and the textual continuity is plausible: the *Śikṣāṣṭakam* states what they elaborate.

engaged_works.title: Doctrine recorded by disciples and biographers
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Caitanya's doctrine survives mainly as recorded by Rūpa, Sanātana, Jīva, and the Caitanya-Caritāmṛta biographer Kṛṣṇadāsa Kavirāja.
engaged_works.summary: Caitanya's doctrine is preserved in three converging streams of disciple-record. The first is biographical: Kṛṣṇadāsa Kavirāja's *Caitanya-Caritāmṛta* and Vṛndāvana-dāsa's *Caitanya-Bhāgavata*, both in Bengali with embedded Sanskrit verse, transmit extended doctrinal dialogues. The most consequential are the conversation with Rāmānanda Rāya on the graded structure of *sādhana* and *rasa* (*Caitanya-Caritāmṛta*, *Madhya-līlā* 8), and the *daśa-mūla* teachings preserved at *Madhya-līlā* 6 (Caitanya–Sārvabhauma) and 20–25 (Caitanya–Sanātana, Caitanya–Rūpa, Caitanya–Prakāśānanda). The second is systematic-theological: the Six Gosvāmīs of Vṛndāvana — Rūpa, Sanātana, Jīva, and the others — articulate Caitanya's position under his commission as *acintya-bhedābheda*, with *acintya-śakti* the load-bearing concept (*Tattva-Sandarbha* §51 ff.) and Jīva Gosvāmī's *Ṣaṭ-Sandarbha* the canonical systematic statement. The third is liturgical-ritual: Sanātana's *Hari-Bhakti-Vilāsa* (twenty *vilāsas* on initiation, deity-worship, and the annual *vrata*-cycle) fixes the school's practice. Reading Caitanya's doctrine therefore requires triangulation across hagiographical, theological, and ritual sources, with the *Śikṣāṣṭakam* (eight verses, transmitted at *Caitanya-Caritāmṛta*, *Antya-līlā* 20) as the only fixed first-person datum. The methodological caution is real but not crippling: the Gosvāmīs treat themselves as drawing out what was settled in Caitanya's teaching, and the doctrinal coherence between the *Śikṣāṣṭakam* and the *Sandarbhas* gives independent warrant for that self-understanding.

cross_engagements.brief: Abhinavagupta presses the same substrate structure axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `one-self-standing` rather than `one-independent-plus-real-dependents`.
Adi Sankara presses the same manifestation status axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `sublatable-not-null` rather than `dependent-real`.
Anandabodha presses the same identity relation axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `non-otherness` rather than `inconceivable-difference-non-difference`.
Akalanka presses the same epistemic authority axis in the epistemological register, but with the value `plural-pramana-realism` rather than `scripture-dominant`.
Anantakrishna Sastri presses the same truth-structure axis in the epistemological register, but with the value `hierarchical-standpoint-truth` rather than `single-absolute-truth`.
Edmund Husserl presses the same perspectivism axis in the epistemological register, but with the value `standpoint-conditioned-realism` rather than `partial-perspectives-ranked`.
Haridas Chaudhuri presses the same motive force axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `bliss-or-delight` rather than `devotion`.
Akshapada Gautama presses the same practice-path axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `knowledge-discipline` rather than `devotion-and-grace`.
Alfred North Whitehead presses the same soteric end axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `not-soteriological` rather than `loving-participation`.
Bannanje Govindacharya presses the same causation model axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `efficient-material-split` rather than `unchanged-ground-with-changing-power`.
G. W. F. Hegel presses the same individuation status axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `reducible-to-the-whole` rather than `irreducible-dependent`.
